title: Novavax Q4 2025 and Full Year Results: Transformation to Partnership Model Drives Profitability
text: Novavax swung to a 2025 net income of $440 million versus a $187 million loss in 2024, on 65% revenue growth to $1.12 billion. Full-year 2025 revenue reached $1.1B, up 65% year-over-year, driven by Nuvaxovid sales and partnerships with Sanofi. Licensing, royalties and other revenue reached $438 million in 2025, including $386 million from Sanofi, where Novavax earned $225 million in milestones for the year. More specifically, Novavax earned $225 million in milestones in full year 2025, including $50 million earned in the fourth quarter of 2025, upon marketing authorization transfers for European Union and U.S. markets. The partnership drove significant operational changes: SG&A expenses for the full year 2025 were $157 million, a 53% decrease compared to $337 million for the full year 2024, primarily due to the transition of lead commercial activities to Sanofi and the elimination of commercial infrastructure. Earnings per share (EPS) of $0.11 exceeded analysts' expectations of -$0.56 by 119.64%, marking a beat. The company transitioned successfully to a partnership-driven business model, dramatically reducing operating expenses while generating substantial milestone and royalty revenues.
